lastReleaseMessage: dashboard: writer emits standardized items-left and backlog item lists

items: Most art assets still outstanding after the native-3D rework
Mission content beyond the first playable

barRaise.topFinding: Cleanest single cycle in the run's recent history: v0.99.11's backlog/dashboard cleanup (17b6043) closed essentially every open finding from 2026-06-24 in one pass. Both carryover MEDs are gone - docs/ARCHITECTURE.md was retired to a 20-line pointer at the GDD Architecture tab + BEST_PRACTICES_PARITY.md, and the world_3d/crowd3d profiler baseline landed in OPTIMIZATION_LOG.md with a real data table. The five trailing LOWs all closed too: outputs/bar_raise_*.py git-rm'd and gitignored, source/vfx/damage_number.gd git-rm'd, the GDD tile-constant note rewritten to closed-for-the-3D-stack, dashboard roster metrics repointed off zero, and the GDD file-map edit committed; the release path itself got hardened against the git-stderr-wrap abort. The one finding that carries forward is the perf signal the new baseline exposed: the 3D crowd holds 67 fps at ~1000 enemies but falls to 34 at 2000 and 22 at 3000, under the S-34 ceiling, with no optimization shipped against it yet.
barRaise.actionsClearedNote: All 4 carryover actions resolved in v0.99.13-v0.99.14. MED CPU frame trace: enemy_field_3d per-record update traced (headless attribution + real windowed desktop trace), dominant op (look_at) gated, ~52% loop reduction, confirmed not the frame bottleneck (render-path-bound); logged in OPTIMIZATION_LOG.md. LOW smoke tests: tests/world3d_subsystems_smoke_test.gd (13 checks over wave_director + economy_system). LOW date pill: auto-stamped in release Step 0 (sync_gdd_date_pill.py). LOW defense-balance: settled as a design decision (footprint growth does not retroactively grant defense), not an open deferral.
